title: BPO: the Bioassay Protocol Ontology, a semantic approach to elevate the reusability of AMR-related drug discovery data
author: Yojana Gadiya
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: To enhance the reproducibility and usability of bioassays in antimicrobial resistance (AMR) associated antibacterial drug discovery and development, there is an increasing need for standardisation of bioassay metadata into machine-readable formats. Such a standardisation process requires mapping bioassay data to standard ontologies and can be performed at the study result output and protocol levels. At the result output level, general ontologies such as the BioAssay Ontology (BAO) already exist. Still, antibacterial drug discovery and AMR-specific ontologies that can aid in the standardisation process at the protocol level are missing. Hence, we propose a new application-specific ontology that will enable researchers to convert unstructured bioassay protocol data into structured machine-readable formats thereby promoting their overall reusability.
steps_raw: To enhance the reproducibility and usability of bioassays in antimicrobial resistance (AMR) associated antibacterial drug discovery and development, there is an increasing need for standardisation of bioassay metadata into machine-readable formats. Such a standardisation process requires mapping bioassay data to standard ontologies and can be performed at the study result output and protocol levels. At the result output level, general ontologies such as the BioAssay Ontology (BAO) already exist. Still, antibacterial drug discovery and AMR-specific ontologies that can aid in the standardisation process at the protocol level are missing. Hence, we propose a new application-specific ontology that will enable researchers to convert unstructured bioassay protocol data into structured machine-readable formats thereby promoting their overall reusability.
